name: Kabardino-Balkaria (Russia)

colors.symbolism: the boundless and clear sky of Kabardino-Balkaria, its rivers and waters; also revered among the Turkic peoples (the Balkars)
peace, purity, and Islam, the traditional religion of the republic
fertility, beauty, the nature of Kabardino-Balkaria; revered by the Kabardians (Adyghe people) and in Islam

description: Horizontal tricolour of light blue, white, and green, with a central circle (light blue above, green below) bearing a white silhouette of Mount Elbrus.
history: Adopted on 21 July 1994. Replaced the 1991-1994 variant of the Russian SFSR flag bearing the republic's name in Russian, Kabardian, and Balkar.

features.symbolism: Mount Elbrus, the highest mountain in Europe, displayed within a circle that crosses light-blue and green halves.
